definition: In top-down analysis, aligning higher timeframe bias, intermediate timeframe structure, and lower timeframe entry signals in the same direction. When all three layers align, price tends to produce strong, sustained moves. Low timeframe structure breaks against HTF trend are likely retracements, not reversals.
identification_rules: Confirm fixed timeframe pairings: Weekly with 4H, Daily with 1H, 4H with 15min, 1H with 5min, 30min with 3min, 15min with 1min.
First layer (bias/HTF): Determine trend direction (higher highs+higher lows for uptrend; lower highs+lower lows for downtrend) or use CISD/protected swing points to establish bias.
Second layer (structure/ITF): Wait for CISD or market structure shift so that structure direction aligns with bias. Mark key levels (Order Block, Fair Value Gap, swing highs/lows).
After structure alignment, wait for price to retrace to key levels (FVG, OB, prior highs/lows).
Third layer (entry/LTF): At the key level, wait for CISD signal or Order Block formation with direction matching structure and bias.
All three timeframes must be aligned in the same direction (both bullish or both bearish); otherwise, no entry.
If LTF structure breaks opposite to HTF trend, treat it as a retracement and check if it falls into HTF PD Array zone.
trading_implication: Enter only when all three layers align; probability of expansion is highest. Place stop at swing high/low of entry timeframe. Target structure or bias layer liquidity levels (e.g., prior day high/low, FVG), typical target 2R. Entry can be taken directly on entry timeframe or drop to lower timeframe for precise entry.
common_mistakes: Only looking at LTF for setups, ignoring ITF and HTF structure confirmation, leading to counter-trend trades.
Entering before ITF structure fully shifts (CISD not confirmed), getting induced out.
Confusing timeframe pairings, e.g., using wrong ITF for HTF alignment.
Entering on LTF signal without waiting for ITF key level to be touched.
Judging trend reversal based solely on LTF lower low while HTF is still uptrend, leading to shorting a retracement.
Trying to trade against HTF bias (e.g., going long on LTF OB when HTF is bearish).
Entering before all three layers are aligned (e.g., structure layer not yet aligned).
merge_notes: Both projects are ICT school; no conflict. The Foundation card provided more detailed identification rules and related concepts; Education - ICT card added aliases in Chinese. Unified card preserves all rules and nuances.
